revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:出雲教]]
{{混同|Izumo Taishakyō}} [[File:御神殿.jpg|thumb|340px|Izumo Taisha Kitajima Kokuzōkan Main Sanctuary]] '''Izumo-kyō''' (出雲教) is a Shintō sect founded in 1882 (Meiji 15) by the 76th {{ill|Izumo clan|en|Izumo clan|qid=Q11395856|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Izumo clan}}, {{ill|Kitajima Nagatane|ja|北島脩孝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitajima Nagatane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who at the time served as a junior chief priest (shōgūji) at {{ill|Izumo-taisha|en|Izumo-taisha|qid=Q696362|lt=Izumo Taisha|1=Izumo-taisha}}, after receiving approval from the Ministry of Home Affairs. The headquarters is located at 194 Taishachō Kizuki Higashi, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture ('''Izumo Taisha Kitajima Kokuzōkan'''). It is a form of {{ill|Shrine Shinto|en|Shrine Shinto|qid=Q1527146|lt=Jinja Shintō|1=Shrine Shinto}} that promotes the divine will and virtues of Ōkuninushi no Ōkami, enshrined at Izumo Taisha.
==Worshipped deities (Saijin)== {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The main deity is {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi no Ōkami|1=Ōkuninushi}}. Other associated deities include the {{ill|Three Creation Kami|ja|造化三神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Creation Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別天津神|1=Kotoamatsukami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q776371}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, {{ill|Ubusuna no Ōkami|ja|産土大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusuna no Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ame no Hohi|en|Ame no Hohi|qid=Q10940685|lt=Ame no Hohi|1=Ame no Hohi}}. Within the precincts, the Tenjinsha shrine enshrines {{ill|Sukunabikona no Kami|ja|少名毘古那神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ|1=Sukunabikona|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3275687}}, the Tenmangū shrine enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|qid=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} and {{ill|Nomi no Sukune|en|Nomi no Sukune|qid=Q965742|lt=Nominosukune|1=Nomi no Sukune}}, the Gosansha consists of shrines to {{ill|Ame no Hohi|en|Ame no Hohi|qid=Q10940685|lt=Ame no Hohi|1=Ame no Hohi}} (ancestor of the Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko), the Kōjinja enshrines {{ill|Kōjin|en|Kōjin|qid=Q6455190|lt=Kōjin|1=Kōjin}} (considered to be Susanoo), and the Inarisha enshrines {{ill|Uka no Mitama|ja|宇迦之御霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka no Mitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
==Overview== Izumo-kyō is a Shintō sect that follows the traditions of ritual inherited by the Kitajima branch of the Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko, descendants of {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}. Its primary aim is to spread the divine virtues of Ōkuninushi no Ōkami, who is enshrined at Izumo Taisha. The sect regards Ame-no-hohi as its founding figure. The first kyōchō (head teacher) was the 76th hereditary kokuzō, Kitajima Nagatane, and the current head is the 80th, Kitajima Takenori.
Kitajima Nagatane was appointed shōgūji of Izumo Taisha in 1871 (Meiji 4), when the shrine was designated as a Kanpei-taisha by Daijō-kan proclamation. In 1882 (Meiji 15), following a ban on priests concurrently serving as doctrinal instructors, he resigned and, with ministry approval, established the "Izumo Kitajima Kyōkai" as a reverent association for Izumo Taisha. The following year, it joined the {{ill|Bureau of Shinto Affairs|en|Bureau of Shinto Affairs|qid=Q11590918|lt=Shintō Jimukyoku|1=Bureau of Shinto Affairs}} (later {{ill|Shintō Taikyō|en|Shintō Taikyō|qid=Q6572869|lt=Shintō Taikyō|1=Shintō Taikyō}}) and was renamed "Shintō Izumo Kyōkai". In 1885 (Meiji 18), it became "Shintō Izumo-kyō", and in 1952 (Shōwa 27), it was reorganized as a religious corporation under the Religious Corporations Act, adopting the name "Izumo-kyō".
Izumo-kyō distinguishes between Izumo Taisha as a shrine and itself as a sect that spreads its spiritual teachings, based on the founding spirit of serving and worshipping Ōkuninushi no Ōkami, deity of the hidden (spiritual) realm. Like the {{ill|Izumo-taishakyo|en|Izumo-taishakyo|qid=Q6102386|lt=Izumo Taishakyō|1=Izumo-taishakyo}} led by the Senge family, it is rooted in Izumo beliefs and has branch institutions across the Chūgoku region and throughout Japan.
==Facilities== ===Main Sanctuary=== Enshrines the primary deity {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi no Ōkami|1=Ōkuninushi}}, as well as the {{ill|Three Creation Kami|ja|造化三神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Creation Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別天津神|1=Kotoamatsukami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q776371}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, {{ill|Ubusuna no Ōkami|ja|産土大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusuna no Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ame no Hohi|en|Ame no Hohi|qid=Q10940685|lt=Ame no Hohi|1=Ame no Hohi}}, ancestor of the Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
===Shrines on the Grounds=== * Tenjinsha – Enshrines {{ill|Sukunabikona no Kami|ja|少名毘古那神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ|1=Sukunabikona|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3275687}}, guardian of agriculture, medicine, hot springs, and sake brewing. * Tenmangū – Enshrines scholar deity {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|qid=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} and sumo deity {{ill|Nomi no Sukune|en|Nomi no Sukune|qid=Q965742|lt=Nominosukune|1=Nomi no Sukune}}, both closely tied to the Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko family. * Gosansha – Comprises Ame-no-hohi Shrine, Kōjinja, and Inarisha. * Sannōsha – Enshrines Ōkunushi no Kami, guardian deity of the land and protector of the northeastern direction. * Soreisha – Enshrines the alternate name of Ōkuninushi, "Kakuriyo Shiroshimesu Ōkami" (Lord of the Hidden Realm), as well as the ancestral spirits of priests and followers.
===Kameyama Kaikan=== Used as a gathering hall, including for post-ritual feasts. Locally known for "Kitajima-san weddings", with ceremonies and receptions often held here. During summer, a seasonal "Kameyama Café" opens, serving Izumo tea and regional sweets to worshippers and visitors.
===Yakumo Kaikan=== Formerly the believers' hall, renovated into a wedding venue. It receives requests from local residents and couples nationwide. Ceremonies for celebrities and athletes have also reportedly been held discreetly. It is also used for lectures and gatherings by affiliated associations.
===Cultural Properties=== * Yotsuashimon Gate – Shimane Prefectural Cultural Property. The main gate of the Kitajima Kokuzō family and the oldest building of Izumo Taisha. * {{ill|Izumo Kagura|ja|出雲神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} "Izumo-kyō Kagura Headquarters, Sannōji Hongō Kagura Troupe" – Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture.
===Kokuzō Residence Garden=== * Garden of the Dragon and Tiger * Heart-shaped Pond and Turtle Tail Waterfall * Statue of {{ill|Sukunabikona no Kami|ja|少名毘古那神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ|1=Sukunabikona|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3275687}} – Said to grant healing and matchmaking if one rubs the stone statue and offers a 100-yen coin. * Aiō no Matsu (Pine of Union) – Believed to bring long-lasting happiness and good matches when omikuji are tied to it. Not currently accessible to the public.
===Surrounding Sites=== * {{ill|Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|en|Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|qid=Q4802099|lt=Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|1=Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo}} * {{ill|Manai Shakeya Street|ja|真名井社家通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manai Shakeya Street|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Izumo no Mori – Though called a forest, it is a sacred place where a giant camphor tree stands surrounded by a sacred fence. The Ryōden Festival (Makomo Ceremony) is held annually on June 1 in front of the sacred tree, praying for safe passage through the troubles of summer. * {{ill|Manai Site|ja|真名井遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manai Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Located behind the Inochinushi Shrine, where bronze swords, spears, and magatama (beads) were excavated in 1953 (Shōwa 28) and later designated Important Cultural Properties. They are on display in the Izumo Taisha Treasure Hall. * Manai no Shimizu – Sacred water used in Izumo Taisha rituals. Long used by locals and considered "Divine Water". Many visitors collect it, and it is among the top 100 waters of Shimane.
==Festivals and Annual Events== * January ''' New Year's Day – Saitan-sai * February ''' Setsubun – Setsubun-sai ''' 17th – Five Grain Bountiful Harvest Prayer Festival / Founder's Festival ''' First Day of the Horse – Hatsuumamatsuri * March ''' Vernal Equinox – Spring Soreisha Annual Festival * May ''' 15th – {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} (Izumo-kyō Grand Festival) * June ''' 1st – Wind and Pestilence Repelling Prayer Festival ''' 30th – Ōharae (Summer Purification) * August ''' 3rd–4th – Tenmangū Festival / Tenjinsha Festival * September ''' 3rd – Recognition Anniversary Festival ''' Autumnal Equinox – Autumn Soreisha Annual Festival * Lunisolar October (Kamiarizuki) ''' 15th – Dragon God Association Festival * November ''' 15th – Himo-otoshi (Shichi-Go-San Ritual) ''' 23rd – Niiname-sai / Mitama Festival * December ''' 15th – Thanksgiving Festival ''' 23rd – Tennō Birthday Celebration ''' 31st – Ōharae / New Year's Eve Festival * Monthly: 1st and 15th – Tsukinamisai * Daily, early morning – Nikusai (Daily Offering Ceremony)
==See Also== * {{ill|Izumo clan|en|Izumo clan|qid=Q11395856|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Izumo clan}} * {{ill|Izumo-taisha|en|Izumo-taisha|qid=Q696362|lt=Izumo Taisha|1=Izumo-taisha}}
==References== * ''Izumo-kyō Yui-sho'' (Izumo Taisha Kitajima Kokuzōkan) * Edited by Kokugakuin University Institute of Japanese Culture: ''Shintō Jiten [Abridged Edition]'' (Kōbundō, 1999) * ''Guide to Izumo Taisha Kitajima Kokuzōkan and Manai Shakeya Street'' by Maniwa Takashi (Izumo Taisha Kitajima Kokuzōkan, Hōkōsha, 2009)
==Contributions to Shintō World== * The Kitajima family of the Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko has ties of kinship with the Imperial Household and has produced personnel who serve the Emperor. * It has produced many priests and staff for renowned shrines across Japan.
==External Links== * [http://www.izumokyou.or.jp/ Izumo Taisha Kitajima Kokuzōkan Izumo-kyō] * [http://www.izumooyashiro.or.jp/ Izumo Taisha]
{{shinto-stub}} {{Shintō Sidebar}}
